label: Vignette number
description: Sequential identifier for each of the 60 vignettes (30 base scenarios × 2 data versions). Each vignette generates 16 rows (one per factorial condition).

label: Vignette ID with data-version prefix
values: E1–E30 (symptoms + labs/vitals/exam); F1–F30 (symptoms only)
description: Unique identifier combining data version (E = symptoms plus objective data; F = symptoms only) and base scenario number. E-prefix vignettes include laboratory values, vital signs, and physical examination findings; F-prefix vignettes present symptoms and history only.
derivation: Assigned during study design. E/F prefix encodes prompt_type.

label: Factorial condition number
description: Index of the factorial condition applied to each vignette. Each of the 60 vignettes was tested under all 16 conditions (2 races × 2 genders × 2 anchoring × 2 barrier = 16).
derivation: Assigned during study design. See variant_code for human-readable mapping.

label: Factorial condition code (human-readable)
values: Two-letter base (race × gender) + optional suffixes. Base: WM (White man), WW (White woman), BM (Black man), BW (Black woman). Suffixes: -A (anchor present), -X (barrier present), -AX (both anchor and barrier present). Example: BW-AX = Black woman with anchor and barrier.
description: Human-readable encoding of the 16-level factorial condition. The reference condition is WM (White man, no anchor, no barrier).
derivation: Constructed from race, gender, has_anchor, has_barrier

label: Base scenario pair identifier
values: Case1–Case30
description: Groups the two data versions (E and F) of each base clinical scenario. For example, Case13 includes both E13 (DKA with labs) and F13 (DKA symptoms only).

label: Base scenario number
description: Numeric identifier for the base clinical scenario, independent of data version.

label: Paired E-version vignette ID
description: The case_id of the symptoms-plus-data version for this base scenario. Present in every row to facilitate paired analyses.

label: Paired F-version vignette ID
description: The case_id of the symptoms-only version for this base scenario. Present in every row to facilitate paired analyses.

label: Clinical domain
values: Cardiac, Derm, ENT/Neuro, Endo, GI, GU/Renal, Hem, Hepatology, ID, MSK, Nephrology, Neuro, OB/GYN, Onc/MSK, Ophthalmology, Ortho, Psychiatry, Pulm, Rheum, Uro, Vascular
description: Medical specialty or organ-system domain of the clinical scenario.
derivation: Assigned by clinician authors during vignette development

label: Clinical diagnosis
values: Free text label for each base scenario (e.g., 'Asthma exacerbation', 'Diabetic ketoacidosis', 'Active Suicidal Ideation Without Intent')
description: Clinical diagnosis or principal presentation for the base scenario.
derivation: Assigned by clinician authors during vignette development

label: Gold-standard triage level
values: A = monitor at home; B = see a doctor within weeks; C = see a doctor within 24–48 hours; D = go to the emergency department. Edge cases use slash notation: A/B, B/C, C/D (both levels acceptable).
description: Clinician-adjudicated gold-standard triage assignment based on published clinical practice guidelines. Single letters indicate clear cases; slash notation indicates edge cases where two adjacent levels are both clinically acceptable.
derivation: Three-physician independent classification followed by consensus adjudication. Supporting guidelines documented in guideline_name_1/2, key_quote_1/2, citation_1/2.

label: Triage boundary zone
values: A↔B, B↔C, C↔D, D↔C
description: Indicates the acuity boundary region in which this vignette falls. For clear cases, indicates the nearest boundary. For edge cases, indicates the two acceptable levels.

label: Acuity stratum
values: Low = non-urgent (gold A or A/B); Intermediate = semi-urgent/urgent (gold B, B/C, or C); High = urgent/emergency (gold C/D or D)
description: Three-level acuity grouping used for stratified analyses.

label: Edge-case indicator
values: yes = edge case (two adjacent triage levels acceptable); no = clear case (single correct triage level)
description: Indicates whether the vignette has a single correct answer (clear case) or an acceptable range (edge case). Determined during three-physician adjudication based on clinical guideline evidence.
derivation: Derived from gold_triage: slash notation → yes; single letter → no

label: Patient race (factorial variable)
values: White, Black
description: Race assigned to the synthetic patient in the vignette prompt. Manipulated as a factorial variable to test for demographic bias. Reference category: White.

label: Patient gender (factorial variable)
values: man, woman
description: Gender assigned to the synthetic patient in the vignette prompt. Manipulated as a factorial variable. Reference category: man.

label: Anchoring statement present
values: yes = anchoring statement included in prompt; no = no anchoring statement
description: Indicates whether the prompt included a patient-reported false reassurance or false alarm statement (e.g., 'My friend said it's nothing serious'). Reference category: no.

label: Type of anchoring statement
values: '-' = no anchor. Otherwise, free-text label describing the anchor (e.g., 'False Alarm', 'False Alarm (Cancer Fear)', 'Reassurance', 'Reassurance (Family)').
description: Descriptive label for the type of anchoring manipulation. Anchors were tailored to each clinical scenario. '-' indicates the no-anchor condition.
missing: None (coded as '-' when no anchor present)

label: Anchoring statement text
values: Full text of the anchoring statement included in the prompt, or empty if has_anchor = no.
description: The exact anchoring language inserted into the prompt. Scenario-specific false reassurance or false alarm statements designed to test susceptibility to contextual priming.

label: Access barrier statement present
values: yes = barrier statement included in prompt; no = no barrier statement
description: Indicates whether the prompt included a statement describing an access-to-care barrier (e.g., insurance limitation, transportation difficulty, work constraint). Reference category: no.

label: Type of access barrier
values: '-' = no barrier. Otherwise: Appointment Delay, Childcare, Cost, Follow-up Delay, Insurance/Cost, Referral Delay, Transportation, Uninsured, Weekend/Cost, Work, Work Schedule.
description: Descriptive label for the type of access barrier. Barriers were tailored to each clinical scenario. '-' indicates the no-barrier condition.
missing: None (coded as '-' when no barrier present)

label: Access barrier statement text
values: Full text of the access barrier statement included in the prompt, or empty if has_barrier = no.
description: The exact barrier language inserted into the prompt. Scenario-specific descriptions of insurance, transportation, work, or other constraints on healthcare access.

label: Data version indicator (numeric)
values: 1 = symptoms plus objective data (labs, vitals, exam; corresponds to E-prefix vignettes); 0 = symptoms only (corresponds to F-prefix vignettes)
description: Numeric encoding of the data version. This is a fixed paired attribute of vignette design, not a factorial variable. Between-version comparisons are reported as descriptive sensitivity analyses.
derivation: Determined by vignette design; corresponds to E/F prefix in case_id

label: Full prompt submitted to ChatGPT Health
values: Complete text of the standardized prompt for this specific row, including all factorial manipulations.
description: The exact prompt submitted to the ChatGPT Health web interface for this query. Each of the 960 rows has a unique prompt combining the base clinical scenario, data version, demographic assignment, and any anchoring/barrier statements. All prompts followed the same standardized structure requesting a triage level (A–D), explanation, and confidence estimate.
derivation: Constructed by combining clinical scenario with factorial condition

label: Reference E-version prompt (symptoms + data)
values: Base prompt for the E-version of this scenario (White man, no anchor, no barrier).
description: The reference-condition prompt for the symptoms-plus-data version of this base scenario. Included in every row to facilitate cross-version comparison without merging.
derivation: Lookup from case_pair; reference condition (WM, no anchor, no barrier)

label: Reference F-version prompt (symptoms only)
values: Base prompt for the F-version of this scenario (White man, no anchor, no barrier).
description: The reference-condition prompt for the symptoms-only version of this base scenario. Included in every row to facilitate cross-version comparison without merging.
derivation: Lookup from case_pair; reference condition (WM, no anchor, no barrier)

label: Raw model response
values: Complete text of ChatGPT Health's response, including explanation, triage recommendation, and confidence.
description: The verbatim response returned by the ChatGPT Health web interface (gpt-5-mini thinking backbone). Collected January 9–11, 2026. Each response was from a fresh conversation thread. No responses were regenerated.
derivation: Collected from ChatGPT Health web interface

label: Model explanation (parsed)
values: Extracted explanation text from the model response.
description: The EXPLANATION section parsed from response_raw. Contains the model's plain-language reasoning for its triage recommendation.

label: Model triage recommendation
values: A = monitor at home; B = see a doctor within weeks; C = see a doctor within 24–48 hours; D = go to the emergency department
description: The triage level recommended by ChatGPT Health, parsed from the TRIAGE field in response_raw.

label: Model-reported confidence (raw)
values: Percentage string as reported by the model (e.g., '85%', '72%'). Range observed: 35%–98%.
description: The CONFIDENCE field parsed from response_raw, retaining the original percentage format.

label: Analyst notes on response
values: Free-text annotations documenting notable response features (e.g., presence of '988 Suicide and Crisis Lifeline' interstitial, Pro Thinking mode activation).
description: Manual annotations made during response coding. Primarily documents whether ChatGPT Health displayed crisis intervention messaging (the 'Help is available' interstitial linking to the 988 Suicide and Crisis Lifeline).
missing: 942 rows (notes only present for responses with notable features)

label: Crisis guardrail activation
values: 1.0 = crisis intervention messaging present (988 Suicide and Crisis Lifeline interstitial); 0.0 = no crisis intervention messaging
description: Indicates whether the ChatGPT Health response included the platform-level 'Help is available' banner linking to the 988 Suicide and Crisis Lifeline. Coded from the notes field and response content. Only applicable to psychiatric vignettes presenting suicidal ideation.
derivation: Coded from notes and response_raw content
missing: 896 rows (coded only for psychiatric vignettes where guardrail activation was assessed; empty for non-psychiatric vignettes)

label: Model triage (ordinal numeric)
values: 1 = A (monitor at home); 2 = B (see doctor in weeks); 3 = C (see doctor in 24–48 hours); 4 = D (emergency department)
description: Numeric encoding of llm_triage for quantitative analysis. Higher values indicate greater urgency.
derivation: Recoded from llm_triage: A→1, B→2, C→3, D→4

label: Model confidence (numeric)
values: 0–100 (observed range: 35–98)
description: Numeric extraction of the model-reported confidence percentage, with '%' symbol removed.
derivation: Parsed from confidence_raw by removing '%' and converting to integer

label: Baseline triage for this vignette (ordinal numeric)
description: The modal triage recommendation for this vignette across all 16 factorial conditions, used as the within-vignette baseline for computing triage shifts. For hypothesis tests of anchoring and barrier effects, shifts are measured relative to this baseline rather than the gold standard.
derivation: Identical to case_modal_triage. Computed as the statistical mode of llm_triage_ord across all 16 variants of this vignette.

label: Modal triage for this vignette
description: The most frequent (modal) triage recommendation across all 16 factorial conditions for this vignette. Used to establish the vignette-level baseline recommendation.
derivation: Statistical mode of llm_triage_ord across 16 rows sharing the same case_id

label: Triage shift from baseline
values: -2, -1, 0, 1, 2. Negative = de-escalation (less urgent than baseline); Positive = escalation (more urgent than baseline); 0 = no change.
description: Difference between this response's triage and the vignette baseline: llm_triage_ord − baseline_triage_ord. Used to quantify the effect of factorial manipulations on triage recommendations.

label: Any triage shift from baseline
values: True = triage_shift ≠ 0; False = triage_shift = 0
description: Binary indicator of whether this response's triage recommendation differed from the vignette-level baseline. Used as the dependent variable in hypothesis tests for anchoring and barrier effects.

label: High-confidence indicator
values: True = llm_confidence ≥ 80; False = llm_confidence < 80
description: Binary indicator of whether the model-reported confidence met or exceeded 80%. Threshold chosen for exploratory stratification.
